attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Graph-Version Fork — FREEZE + REMOVAL-GATES (3c)
2	
3	**Scope:** Review-readiness inventory only. Nothing deleted, no behavior changed.
4	This document FREEZES the `PG_GRAPH_VERSION` graph fork and defines the gates
5	that must ALL pass before any `graph*.py` version is removed.
6	
7	**Baseline:** collection `16738/11`.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 1. STATUS — FROZEN
12	
13	The graph fork (`graph.py` / `graph_v2.py` / `graph_v3.py` / `pipeline_a_ui_adapter`
14	via `PG_GRAPH_VERSION`) is **FROZEN** for this review — **documented, not deleted.**
15	
16	The brief described a 3-way fork. It is in fact a **5-way selector** (v4 / v3 / v2 / v1
17	+ safe fallback), and a **v4 pipeline-A-backed path is now the production DEFAULT.**
18	
19	- **Default = v4** (`pipeline_a_ui_adapter.build_and_run_v4`, pipeline-A-backed) on the
20	  production `live_server` path. **Unset `PG_GRAPH_VERSION` => v4.**
21	- The library `__init__.py` selector defaults to **v1**, but it is **NOT on the
22	  production path** (dormant — see §2).
23	
24	### Selector routing (production: `scripts/live_server.py:555-570`, `_run_pipeline`)
25	
26	Reads `os.getenv("PG_GRAPH_VERSION", "v4")`:
27	
28	| Selector value | Routes to |
29	|---|---|
30	| `v4` (default / unset) | `pipeline_a_ui_adapter.build_and_run_v4` |
31	| `v3` | `graph_v3.build_and_run_v3` |
32	| `v2` | `graph_v2.build_and_run` |
33	| `v1` | `graph.build_and_run` |
34	| any-other-value | **v4 safe fallback** (logs a warning) |
35	| `PG_V2_ENABLED=1` | forces `graph_v2` — a **second, independent** selector env var (default `'0'`, read directly, not in `config_defaults`) |
36	
37	The `PG_V2_ENABLED=1` branch is evaluated **before** `v1` and independently of
38	`PG_GRAPH_VERSION`, so `PG_V2_ENABLED=1` overrides `PG_GRAPH_VERSION=v1`.
39	
40	### Second selector site (DORMANT — not production)
41	
42	`src/polaris_graph/__init__.py:37-41` reads `resolve("PG_GRAPH_VERSION")`
43	(registry default `'v1'` — `config_defaults.py:365`) and distinguishes only
44	`v3 -> graph_v3` else `v1 -> graph.build_and_run`. This entry point
45	(`src.polaris_graph.run_research`) has **no runtime caller** — production scripts
46	import `src.orchestration.graph.run_research`, a different module. The two selector
47	sites **DISAGREE** on the default (`live_server`=v4, `__init__.py`=v1) and on routing
48	breadth.
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 2. USAGE INVENTORY
53	
54	### Which selector is production
55	`scripts/live_server.py:555-570`. Docker `CMD serve` -> `live_server` always resolves
56	to **v4** unless `PG_GRAPH_VERSION` is explicitly overridden (it never is — see below).
57	
58	### Is any non-default (v2/v3/v1) used anywhere?
59	**NO.** `nondefault_usage_found = false` at runtime, anywhere:
60	
61	- No Dockerfile / entrypoint / shell / CI / `.env` / config sets `PG_GRAPH_VERSION`
62	  or `PG_V2_ENABLED` (grep over `*.sh`, `Dockerfile*`, `*.yml`, `*.yaml`, `*.env`
63	  returns nothing).
64	- `tests/v3/test_graph.py:189-196` only asserts env-var round-trip — **not** real routing.
65	- `tests/polaris_graph/test_b102_graph_v4.py` is a source-assertion test that greps
66	  `live_server` for the v4 literal.
67	- External consumers of `graph_v2` / `graph_v3` / `build_and_run_v4` symbols =
68	  **ONLY the two selector sites** (`live_server.py:557,559,561,570` and `__init__.py:39`).
69	  No other runtime module imports `graph_v2` / `graph_v3` / `ResearchStateV2` / `V3State` /
70	  `build_and_run_v4`.
71	
72	Production therefore always runs **v4**; v1/v2/v3 are reachable only by a manual
73	env override that no deployment performs.
74	
75	### State classes
76	
77	| Version | State class | Location | Persistence |
78	|---|---|---|---|
79	| v1 | `ResearchState` (TypedDict) | `src/polaris_graph/state.py:469` | SQLite checkpointer (`checkpoint_manager.get_checkpointer`, `graph.py:1421-1437`, `PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED`) + final JSON `outputs/polaris_graph/{vector_id}.json` |
80	| v2 | `ResearchStateV2` (TypedDict) | `src/polaris_graph/graph_v2.py:71` | **NO checkpointer** (`graph.compile()` @ `graph_v2.py:678`); final JSON dump `graph_v2.py:840-841` |
81	| v3 | `V3State` (TypedDict) | `src/polaris_graph/state_v3.py:15` | evidence content off-state in side-channel `evidence_store`; **NO checkpointer** (`graph_v3.py:705`); final JSON `PG_OUTPUT_DIR/{vector_id}.json` `graph_v3.py:843-851` |
82	| v4 | **NO own State class** | `pipeline_a_ui_adapter.build_and_run_v4` (`py:187`) delegates to `scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query` | final JSON dump `pipeline_a_ui_adapter.py:335` |
83	
84	Dead artifact: a commented-out `ResearchStateV2` sketch at `state.py:375` — left as-is.
85	
86	**Persistence note:** only **v1** uses the LangGraph SQLite checkpointer. v2 / v3 / v4
87	only write a final result JSON. This matters for Gate (c) — saved-state resume fixtures
88	only exist meaningfully for v1.
89	
90	---
91	
92	## 3. DEPRECATION NOTICE
93	
94	The non-default forks — **v1 (`graph.py`), v2 (`graph_v2.py`), v3 (`graph_v3.py`),
95	and `PG_V2_ENABLED`** — are hereby marked **DEPRECATED**.
96	
97	**Removal is a SEPARATE, owner-driven project (codex Q1 decision).** This review does
98	**not** delete them. Deprecation here means: no new work targets these paths; they exist
99	only for migration compatibility; they are slated for removal once §4 gates pass.
100	
101	---
102	
103	## 4. REMOVAL GATES (codex-specified)
104	
105	**ALL of the following must pass before ANY `graph*.py` version is deleted:**
106	
107	- **(a) Full-graph deterministic oracle** covering EVERY `PG_GRAPH_VERSION` selector,
108	  including **unset** and **invalid** (safe-fallback) values, plus the `PG_V2_ENABLED=1`
109	  branch.
110	- **(b) Byte-identical RACE + faithfulness** across repeated replays per selector.
111	- **(c) Saved-state migration + resume fixtures** for `ResearchStateV2` and `V3State`

"""W1 acceptance harness for the agentic outliner loop (I-outline-agent).

Runs TWO real, live scenarios against src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:

  (a) THIN  — seed evidence covers ONLY part of the research question (efficacy), the question
      itself also asks about long-term cardiovascular safety. Acceptance: the checklist names
      the gap, search_more_evidence fires a real scoped query, new rows fold in with
      collision-free ids + S2 stamps, the outline mutates (ev_ids grow / new gap-covering rows
      cited).

  (b) SATURATED — a narrow question whose seed evidence already fully covers it (negative
      control). Acceptance: retrieval stays at ZERO (no search_more_evidence tool call ever
      fires) — the checklist should find nothing to search for and finish_outline should be
      accepted quickly.

FAIL LOUD: any exception is allowed to propagate and print a full traceback — this harness never
swallows an error to fake a pass (LAW II).
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path

# PORTABLE COPY of acceptance_outline_agent.py (the four /workspace/... hardcodes replaced with
# repo-relative / env-var-configurable paths). This file lives at <repo>/tests/oracle/, so the
# repo root is three parents up. Behaviour of the harness is otherwise byte-identical to the
# original — only path resolution changed, so it can serve as the governing oracle from any
# checkout location. See ORACLE_PORTABILITY note at the bottom for the four substitutions.
_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]

sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))

async def main() -> None:
    # Iter-2: allow re-verifying a single scenario (``--only thin`` / ``--only saturated``) so a
    # targeted fix to one path doesn't force re-spending on the other, already-passing scenario.
    # Default (no arg) runs BOTH, unchanged, and writes the full acceptance_result.json.
    only = None
    if "--only" in sys.argv:
        idx = sys.argv.index("--only")
        if idx + 1 < len(sys.argv):
            only = sys.argv[idx + 1].strip().lower()

    mode = _parse_mode()
    if mode is not None:
        await _main_oracle(mode, only)
        return

    result = await _run_scenarios(only)
    print("\n" + "#" * 80)
    print("ACCEPTANCE SUMMARY")
    print("#" * 80)
    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))
    # (4) output dir: env override PG_ACCEPTANCE_OUT_DIR wins; else the repo root (mirrors the
    #     original which wrote into /workspace/outline_agent_wt, i.e. the repo root of the old
    #     container layout). Dir is created if absent.
    out_dir = Path(os.environ.get("PG_ACCEPTANCE_OUT_DIR", str(_REPO_ROOT)))
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out_name = "acceptance_result.json" if only is None else f"acceptance_result_{only}.json"
    out_path = out_dir / out_name
    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        json.dump(result, fh, indent=2, default=str)
    print(f"[portable] wrote {out_path}")

    # Enforce BOTH controls: the harness must exit NON-ZERO if the positive (THIN) control did not
    # fire a search + mutate the outline, or if the negative (SATURATED) control did not stay at
    # zero retrieval on a genuinely-built loop. A scenario that was not run this invocation
    # (``--only``) is skipped, not treated as a failure.
    failures: list[str] = []
    if "thin" in result and not result["thin"].get("valid_positive_control"):
        failures.append("positive control (THIN): valid_positive_control=False")
    if "saturated" in result and not result["saturated"].get("valid_negative_control"):
        failures.append("negative control (SATURATED): valid_negative_control=False")
    if failures:
        print("\nACCEPTANCE FAILED:")
        for f in failures:
            print(f"  - {f}")
        sys.exit(1)
    print("\nACCEPTANCE PASSED: all run controls valid")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

394	
395	    def _patched(self, include_results: bool = False) -> str:
396	        # Normalize BOTH wall-clock timings the notebook feeds the decide prompt, at their SOURCE so
397	        # the downstream length-sensitive truncation (_result_digest_lines caps the flattened result
398	        # markdown at a char budget) sees byte-identical text in record and replay:
399	        #   (a) the per-step ``({elapsed:.1f}s)`` in the step header line — zero elapsed_seconds; and
400	        #   (b) the ``... in {elapsed:.1f}s`` that search_more_evidence bakes INTO its ToolResult
401	        #       .markdown (outline_agent.py:821/825). Record's raw markdown carries e.g. "in 84.4s"
402	        #       (7 chars); replay's carries "in 0.0s" (6 chars). If we normalized only the FINAL
403	        #       summary the digest budget would already have truncated one char differently — the
404	        #       classic off-by-one that made the turn-2 decide prompt differ by a single character.
405	        #       So we rewrite each step's result.markdown to the fixed-width ``in <elapsed>s`` token
406	        #       BEFORE _orig renders/truncates it. Both are non-behavioural timings; the identical
407	        #       normalization in record and replay never hides a real change.
408	        saved_elapsed = [s.elapsed_seconds for s in self._steps]
409	        saved_md = [getattr(s.result, "markdown", None) for s in self._steps]
410	        try:
411	            for s in self._steps:
412	                s.elapsed_seconds = 0.0
413	                md = getattr(s.result, "markdown", None)
414	                if isinstance(md, str):
415	                    s.result.markdown = _ELAPSED_IN_DISCLOSURE.sub(" in <elapsed>s", md)
416	            summary = _orig(self, include_results)
417	        finally:
418	            for s, ev, md in zip(self._steps, saved_elapsed, saved_md):
419	                s.elapsed_seconds = ev
420	                if md is not None:
421	                    s.result.markdown = md
422	        return _ELAPSED_IN_DISCLOSURE.sub(" in <elapsed>s", summary)
423	
424	    _an.AnalysisNotebook.summary_for_llm = _patched
425	    try:
426	        yield
427	    finally:
428	        _an.AnalysisNotebook.summary_for_llm = _orig

432	    """Run the acceptance scenarios with the LLM and/or retrieval boundaries frozen, emit the
433	    golden artifact, and (in replay) enforce byte-identity + the two controls."""
434	    from tests.oracle.llm_cassette import llm_cassette  # noqa: PLC0415

436	    import hashlib  # noqa: PLC0415

446	        raise SystemExit(f"unknown --mode {mode!r} (use seed-retrieval | record | replay)")
447	
448	    print(f"[oracle] mode={mode} retrieval-cassette={retr_mode} llm-cassette={llm_mode}")
449	    print(f"[oracle] retrieval tape: {_RETRIEVAL_TAPE}")
450	    print(f"[oracle] llm tape:       {_LLM_TAPE}")
451	
452	    _CASSETTE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
453	
454	    # Order matters: retrieval cassette is the OUTER context so its patched run_live_retrieval is in
455	    # place before any scenario runs; the llm cassette patches the OpenRouter boundary independently.
456	    # _frozen_step_clock normalizes the per-step elapsed the notebook feeds the decide prompt (a
457	    # non-behavioural wall-clock input) so record and replay issue byte-identical decide requests.
458	    with _frozen_step_clock(), \

import ast, importlib.util, os, sys

rfs = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(rfs)

def extract_env_dict(path, var_name):
    """Statically evaluate the _VNN_ENV literal from an original script."""
    tree = ast.parse(path.read_text())
    for node in ast.walk(tree):
        if isinstance(node, ast.AnnAssign) and getattr(node.target, "id", None) == var_name:
            return ast.literal_eval(node.value)
        if isinstance(node, ast.Assign):
            for t in node.targets:
                if getattr(t, "id", None) == var_name:
                    return ast.literal_eval(node.value)
    raise KeyError(var_name)

def extract_str(path, needle_prefix, group):
    """Grab out-root / banner from original via simple literal scan."""
    tree = ast.parse(path.read_text())
    found = []
    for node in ast.walk(tree):
        if isinstance(node, ast.Constant) and isinstance(node.value, str):
            found.append(node.value)
    return found

variants = ["v10","v23","v24","v25","v26","v27","v28","v29","v30_phase2"]

    orig_env = extract_env_dict(op, env_var[v])
    shim_env = rfs.VARIANT_ENV[v]

    # dict equality is order-insensitive but also verify key set + values explicitly
    keys_ok = set(orig_env) == set(shim_env) and all(orig_env[k]==shim_env[k] for k in orig_env)

calls = []
def fake_run(variant, argv):

rfs.run = fake_run  # patch the symbol the shims import at call-time? They do `from run_full_scale import run`

# Because shims do `from run_full_scale import run` at import, they bind the name.
# To intercept, we must patch BEFORE importing the shim module.

    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
    # rebind the shim's local `run` to our spy
    mod.run = fake_run
    sys.argv = [f"{modname}.py", "--out-root", "X", "--only", "Y"]
    rc = mod.main()
    assert rc == 0, f"{v} main rc={rc}"
    got_variant, got_argv = calls[-1]
    assert got_variant == v, f"{v}: shim delegated with variant={got_variant!r}"
    assert got_argv == ["--out-root", "X", "--only", "Y"], f"{v}: argv={got_argv}"

=====codex_decisions.md=====
# DECISION REQUEST — you are the decision-maker (GPT-5.6-Sol, max reasoning)

Context: a "code-review-readiness" cleanup of a deep-research pipeline (Deep Cove Research) for an independent Telus review. 14 PRs already shipped, every change gated by a deterministic oracle (byte-identical RACE score + faithfulness). TWO ABSOLUTE RULES held throughout and must continue: (A) faithfulness behavior FROZEN — never weaken a threshold/verifier; (B) no change may move the pipeline's RACE score. A trustworthy oracle exists but only covers the OUTLINE-AGENT path (THIN+SATURATED), not the full graph. Env overrides win at runtime (os.getenv > .env > code default).

For EACH question below, give a DECISION (not just analysis): a clear verdict + one-line rationale + any safety condition. Be decisive.

## Q1 — 3C: remove the live 3-way graph fork (graph.py / graph_v2 / graph_v3, selected by PG_GRAPH_VERSION)?
Plan V4 calls this "the single most dangerous change" and "its own reviewed change." It needs: per-selector compatibility fixtures (0A-5) which require running the FULL langgraph pipeline deterministically (providers/GPU/browser — the same hard non-determinism work the outline-oracle needed, which took a large effort); a usage inventory; ResearchStateV2 saved-state migration fixtures; repeated replays; rollback + deprecation window. The current oracle does NOT cover the graph path.
DECIDE: (a) attempt 3C removal now, (b) defer 3C entirely to a separate owner-driven effort, or (c) a middle path (e.g. document + freeze the fork, add a usage inventory + deprecation notice, but do NOT delete). Give the recommended scope + the minimum safety gates required before any deletion.

## Q2 — PG_GENERATOR_MODEL conflicting default
It's read with conflicting fallbacks ('' vs no-default) at different sites. It IS set in .env (=z-ai/glm-5.2, env wins at runtime, so aligning code defaults is byte-identical to current runtime). The only open question is the fallback when the env var is UNSET.
DECIDE: what fallback default should all call sites use? Options: (a) '' (empty → 'auto/unset' sentinel, matches most sites), (b) the deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro code default seen at one site, (c) leave as owner-only. Note the byte-safety (env set → runtime unchanged regardless).

## Q3 — 16 DOMAIN-REVIEW rename rows (keep vs rename)
The worklist flagged these for owner judgment. On inspection MOST are legitimate domain/technical terms already marked KEEP-NOTED: DeepTRACE (published metric), G-Eval (standard LLM-eval technique), SmartArt (real feature), "chrome" (browser-UI domain term), "tunnel view" (idiomatic), FORCE_APPROVE_RE (documented emergency-override). A few are genuine RENAME candidates, mostly in RETIRED or benchmark scripts: lethal_retrieve (in scripts/_retired_2026_06_14/), aggregate_beat_both_runs.py + BEAT-BOTH (an established internal benchmark taxonomy used consistently).
DECIDE: policy for the 16 — (a) KEEP all (they're legitimate domain vocabulary; renaming would obscure real technique names), (b) rename only the marketing-adjective ones ("lethal") and keep the real technique names, or (c) rename all. Which specific ones (if any) to rename.

## Q4 — NEEDS-ALIAS canonical names (the worklist gave CONFLICTING suggested targets)
These are control-surface/persisted-string symbols; the rule is rename the symbol but keep the old string working via an alias. The worklist's own suggestions DISAGREE, so a human must pick the canonical new name:
- HonestSweepJobRunner / HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig / make_default_honest_sweep_job_runner: suggested SweepJobRunner (re-export site) vs V30SweepJobRunner (definition site). The runner is specific to a "v30" clinical sweep template (dispatch key template_id='v30_clinical').
- is_row_content_junk: suggested is_row_content_low_quality vs is_row_content_integrity_violation. It screens fabricated/low-integrity evidence rows.
- junk_deletion_gate.py (module): suggested nonsource_deletion_gate vs content_integrity_deletion_gate vs off_topic_deletion_gate. It deletes evidence rows that fail a content-integrity/source screen.
DECIDE: the single canonical new name for each cluster. Also DECIDE the operator-consumer safety policy: since a repo grep cannot prove no external deployment consumes a renamed run-ID prefix / output filename / env string, should the default be "add a backward-compat alias for EVERY control-surface/persisted string rename" (safe but adds shim code) or "rename only after confirming zero external consumers"?

## Q5 — SecretStr pass
Wrapping ~50-56 secret-shaped config keys in pydantic SecretStr (call sites use .get_secret_value()). The S4 security threat model already found secrets are NEVER logged as values (good hygiene). SecretStr is invasive (touches every secret call site) and behavior-adjacent.
DECIDE: (a) do the SecretStr pass, (b) skip it (log hygiene already good, low marginal value, invasive), or (c) a lighter alternative (e.g. a masked-repr wrapper only at log boundaries).

## Q6 — remaining low-risk items: priority order
Remaining: the run_full_scale_v10..v30 launcher consolidation (behavior-preserving, was split out for a separate tested commit); the flakiness empirical N-run (suite too slow to run 3x in one window, ~12min/run, not concurrency-safe — Plan V4 says "over several days"); computed-default config keys → typed accessors (46 keys); more docstrings toward ~530.
DECIDE: recommended priority order for these, and which (if any) are not worth doing for a code review.

End each answer with the decision on its own line prefixed "DECISION Qn: ...".
